name: Dark Utility
tagline: Purposeful dark utility style with near-black surfaces, a single muted accent, and product-first restraint.

palette.description: Deep charcoal near-black background; white/light-gray text for high contrast; single muted blue or cyan accent for interactive elements and icons; no decorative color.

typography.style: Modern geometric sans-serif. Clean, neutral, functional. Two levels of hierarchy — large bold headlines, small clear body text. No decorative type.

best_for: Tool and utility app showcases, open-source software presentations, developer-focused products that want to feel as purposeful as the tool itself. Works for technical reviews, feature summaries, and tool comparison decks.
avoid_for: Consumer lifestyle products, warm personal narratives, anything requiring color expressiveness or warmth. Don't use if the content needs visual excitement.
anti_patterns: adding decorative illustrations or marketing graphics — authenticity comes from showing the real product
using more than 2 accent colors — fewer colors = stronger tool identity
making the page too long — utility users want fast judgment, keep it short and precise
adding heavy animations — the style's credibility depends on functionality-first feel
